title: Letter IEDI n. 1292—Brazil-China trade and the loss of Brazilian ...
description: Unlike China, the complexity of Brazilian exports, which was already low, has decreased in recent years, lowering our position in the world ranking of complexity and making a long-term growth trajectory more difficult. Versão em português deste texto.
extracted:
### Trade Volumes and Shares
- **2023 total Brazil-China trade**: US$157.5 billion (record in MDIC historical series)
- **2023 Brazilian exports to China**: >US$100 billion (first time surpassing this mark)
- **2024 trade flow (Jan–Nov)**: US$147.8 billion
- **2024 Brazilian imports from China (Jan–Nov)**: +20.4% vs. Jan–Nov 2023
- **China's share in Brazilian trade**: >1/4 of international exchanges
- **1997–2000 average**:
  - China share of Brazilian exports: 1.8%
  - China share of Brazilian imports: 1.9%
- **2023**:
  - China share of Brazilian exports: 30.7%
  - China share of Brazilian imports: 22.1%
- **2019–2023**:
  - Brazilian exports to China: +64.7% (current values)
  - Chinese exports to Brazil: +47.6% (current values)

### Industrial Sectors Impacted
- Stronger Chinese presence in Brazilian imports in: base metals, vehicles and auto parts, chemicals, machinery and equipment

### Export Basket Composition (Brazil, 2014–2021)
| Category/Product | 2014 Share | 2021 Share |
|------------------|------------|------------|
| Mineral and agricultural products (total) | 54.3% | 65.2% |
| Vehicles | 5.9% | 3.6% |
| Machinery | 4.6% | 3.6% |
| Chemical products | 6.4% | 5.0% |

### Brazil Exports to China (Concentration)
- **2014 and 2021**: Concentrated in three products (iron ores, soybeans, crude oil), representing ~80% of total exports to China

### Export Basket Composition (China, 2014–2021)
| Category | 2014 Share | 2021 Share |
|----------|------------|------------|
| Electronics and machinery (two main types, total) | 44% | 47.2% |

### Revealed Comparative Advantages (2021)
- **China**: 391 products
- **Brazil**: 124 products

### Economic Complexity Index (ECI) and Rankings
- **Brazil ECI evolution**: Significant and persistent worsening
  - 1995 world ranking: 25th
  - 2021 world ranking: 70th
- **Regional comparisons (1995–2021 rankings)**:
  - Argentina, Chile, Colombia, Paraguay: Lost positions (less intensely than Brazil)
  - Mexico: 29th to 22nd
- **China ECI and rankings**:
  - 1995: 46th
  - 2000: 30th
  - 2010: 24th
  - 2014: 18th (ECI: 1.32)
  - 2021: 18th (ECI: 1.33)

### Data Sources and Period
- Sources: Atlas of Complexity, Trademap, Comtrade
- Period: 2014–2021 (prior to 2015/2016 crisis and COVID-19; limited by database availability)
